title: Zscaler Browser Isolation Usage
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:isolation"
| stats count by user, url_category
| sort -count
description: Browser isolation usage shows adoption of zero-trust browsing for untrusted sites.
value: Browser isolation usage shows adoption of zero-trust browsing for untrusted sites.
implementation: Track isolation session starts for risky categories. Validate policy covers targeted users.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Zscaler blocks from category misclassifications, new SaaS rollouts, or short policy-tightening windows; triage with app owners and DLP before assuming compromise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zscaler:isolation`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Track isolation session starts for risky categories. Validate policy covers targeted users.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:isolation"
| stats count by user, url_category
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Zscaler Browser Isolation Usage** ŌĆö Browser isolation usage shows adoption of zero-trust browsing for untrusted sites.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:isolation`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: zscaler:isolation. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP ŌĆö interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype="zscaler:isolation". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, url_category** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Zscaler Browser Isolation Usage** ŌĆö Browser isolation usage shows adoption of zero-trust browsing for untrusted sites.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:isolation`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP ŌĆö interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` ŌĆö enable acceleration for that model.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendorŌĆÖs own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (categories), Table (users).
grandmaExplanation: We use Zscaler data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Zscaler Browser Isolation Usage". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Web.Web` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
